response.justification: Bogdan brings 20+ years of polyglot engineering experience and holds a directly relevant credential: hands-on work building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, which maps tightly to the role's core delivery responsibilities. However, the resume contains no mention of Python — the primary language for GenAI tooling — nor of RAG architectures, vector databases, or LLM evaluation pipelines, all of which are explicit requirements. His cloud exposure is narrow (AWS CDK for a pricing module at Optilyz) and his customer-facing, forward-deployed consulting experience is not demonstrated. The AI work at re:cinq is described in high-level terms without concrete deliverables, making it hard to assess depth. A screening call to probe the AI infrastructure work and Python proficiency would clarify whether this is a strong fit or a surface-level match.
strengths: Directly relevant MCP server development and agentic workflow infrastructure experience at re:cinq aligns with the role's production AI delivery mandate
Staff-level tenure across multiple companies with TypeScript/Node.js at scale demonstrates the seniority and breadth expected for a forward-deployed lead
Long track record of teaching, workshops, and open-source contribution signals the mentorship and engineering-excellence responsibilities the role requires
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills section and resume narrative — a critical gap for a GenAI role where Python is the dominant ecosystem language
No mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI), which are core required and preferred qualifications
Customer-facing, forward-deployed consulting experience is not evidenced; the role demands leading technical discovery with enterprise stakeholders, and the resume lacks examples of this
